chief_complaint: A 49-year-old man presented to the emergency department with epigastric pain and nausea.
history: The patient presented with epigastric pain and nausea. Six hours later, he experienced severe chest pain. There is no mention of past medical history, medications, or social history.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: Initial electrocardiogram was normal, but a repeat electrocardiogram 6 hours later demonstrated inferior ST-segment elevation. Troponin I levels peaked at 1.2 ng/ml. Coronary angiography revealed angiographic stenosis in the left circumflex territory which resolved with intracoronary nitroglycerin administration.
correct_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome due to coronary vasospasm
differentials: Acute coronary syndrome due to coronary vasospasm
correct_ranking: Acute coronary syndrome due to coronary vasospasm
